content.summaryText: Oral Rehydration Therapy in Gastrointestinal Infections focuses on dehydration risk assessment and rehydration planning. Primary objective: Prepare ORS correctly.

content.questionText: In Oral Rehydration Therapy, which action best supports 'Prepare ORS correctly'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Estimate fluid deficit and start oral or IV rehydration appropriately
Delayed fluid replacement while waiting for confirmatory testing
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: estimate fluid deficit and start oral or IV rehydration appropriately. Practical emphasis: Dose by weight and severity.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when severe dehydration, blood in stool, persistent vomiting, or shock.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
